description: Main psychological definitions and formulas that are not part of the units, physics, math or chemistry base data.

words.name: psychology
words.description: study of mental functions and behaviours

words.name: clinical psychology
words.description: integration of science and clinical knowledge for the purpose of relieving psychologically based dysfunction

words.name: cognitive psychology
words.description: subdiscipline of psychology

words.name: developmental psychology
words.description: scientific study of changes that occur in human beings over the course of their lives

words.name: behaviorism
words.description: approach to psychology

words.name: neuropsychology
words.description: study of the brain related to specific psychological processes and behaviors

words.name: educational psychology
words.description: branch of psychology concerned with the scientific study of human learning

words.name: industrial and organizational psychology
words.description: branch of psychology

words.name: personality psychology
words.description: branch of psychology focused on personality

words.name: abnormal psychology
words.description: branch of psychology that studies unusual patterns of behavior, emotion, and thought, which could possibly be understood as a mental disorder

words.name: health psychology
words.description: the study of psychological and behavioral processes in health, illness, and healthcare

words.name: positive psychology
words.description: scientific study of the positive aspects of the human experience that make life worth living

words.name: evolutionary psychology
words.description: branch of psychology

words.name: psychometrics
words.description: study of the theory and technique of psychological measurement

words.name: forensic psychology
words.description: the intersection between psychology and the justice system

words.name: psychophysics
words.description: scientific study of perceptual systems

words.name: cognition
words.description: mental action or process of acquiring knowledge and understanding through thought, experience, and the senses

words.name: memory
words.description: mental faculties and processes involved in storing and retrieving information

words.name: perception
words.description: organization, identification, and interpretation of sensory information in order to represent and understand the environment in the brain

words.name: attention
words.description: behavioral and cognitive process of selectively concentrating on a discrete aspect of information, whether deemed subjective or objective, while ignoring other perceivable information

words.name: reasoning
words.description: the capacity of consciously making sense of things, applying logic, and adapting or justifying practices, institutions, and beliefs based on new or existing information

words.name: thought
words.description: mental activity involving an individual's subjective consciousness

words.name: consciousness
words.description: knowledge that a being has of its external and internal existence and its environment

words.name: creativity
words.description: cognitive process whereby something new and somehow valuable is formed

words.name: short-term memory
words.description: the memory process that deals with the storage, retrieval and modification of information received a short time (up to about 30 minutes) ago

words.name: long-term memory
words.description: memory process that deals with the storage, retrieval and modification of information a long time (typically weeks, months or years) after receiving that information

words.name: working memory
words.description: cognitive system with a limited capacity that is responsible for temporarily holding information available for processing

words.name: sensory memory
words.description: bodily experienced sensations from the past

words.name: encoding
words.description: memory process

words.name: recall
words.description: retrieval of events or information from the past

words.name: recognition
words.description: subcategory of declarative memory, referring to the ability to recognize previously encountered events, objects, or people

words.name: forgetting
words.description: loss or failure to recall information stored in human memory

words.name: cognitive bias
words.description: systematic pattern of deviation from norm or rationality in judgment due to subjective perception of reality

words.name: heuristic
words.description: simple strategies, rules or mental processes involved in making quick judgments or decisions

words.name: confirmation bias
words.description: tendency of people to favor information that confirms their beliefs or hypotheses

words.name: gestalt psychology
words.description: theory of mind examining human perception, structures and organizing principles in sensory impressions

words.name: depth perception
words.description: visual ability to perceive the world in three dimensions (3D)

words.name: optical illusion
words.description: distortion of the senses or perception of reality, which may reveal how the human brain normally organizes and interprets sensory stimulation

words.name: behavior
words.description: range of actions by entities, living or not, within a system

words.name: learning
words.description: process of acquiring new knowledge

words.name: reinforcement
words.description: consequence that will strengthen an organism's future behavior whenever that behavior is preceded by a specific antecedent stimulus

words.name: punishment
words.description: facet of operant conditioning; change in a human or animal's surroundings that occurs after a given behavior or response which reduces the likelihood of that behavior occurring again in the future

words.name: classical conditioning
words.description: learning procedure in which biologically potent stimulus is paired with a neutral stimulus

words.name: operant conditioning
words.description: learning to anticipate future events on the basis of past experience with the consequences of one's own behavior

words.name: habituation
words.description: decrease in a behavioral response to a repeated stimulus

words.name: extinction
words.description: fading of non-reinforced conditioned response over time

words.name: stimulus
words.description: in psychology, an object or event that elicits a sensory or behavioral response in an organism

words.name: attachment theory
words.description: psychological ethological theory about human relationships

words.name: cognitive development
words.description: field of study in neuroscience and psychology

words.name: object permanence
words.description: understanding that objects continue to exist even when they cannot be observed

words.name: nature versus nurture
words.description: relative importance of an individual's innate qualities ("nature" in the sense of nativism or innatism) as compared to an individual's personal experiences ("nurture" in the sense of empiricism or behaviorism)

words.name: temperament
words.description: term used to indicate the mixture of the innate aspects of the personality

words.name: attitude
words.description: psychological construct, a mental and emotional entity that inheres in, or characterizes a person

words.name: cognitive dissonance
words.description: mental stress or discomfort experienced by an individual who holds two or more contradictory beliefs, ideas, or values at the same time

words.name: obedience
words.description: yielding to instructions from an authority figure

words.name: bystander effect
words.description: social psychological phenomenon in which individuals are less likely to offer help to a victim when other people are present

words.name: social influence
words.description: alteration of attitudes and behaviors based on outside influences

words.name: persuasion
words.description: mode of social communication that aims to influence someone else's beliefs, attitudes, or intentions

words.name: attribution
words.description: process by which individuals explain the causes of behavior and events

words.name: empathy
words.description: capacity to understand or feel what another person is experiencing from within the latter's frame of reference

words.name: personality
words.description: psychological characteristics of an individual (usually referring to only humans)

words.name: trait theory
words.description: approach to the study of human personality

words.name: Big Five personality traits
words.description: personality model consisting of five broad dimensions of personality

words.name: extraversion
words.description: personality trait

words.name: neuroticism
words.description: personality trait of the tendency to experience negative emotions

words.name: self-concept
words.description: one's internal beliefs about oneself

words.name: self-esteem
words.description: term used in psychology to reflect a person's overall emotional evaluation of their own worth

words.name: mental disorder
words.description: distressing thought or behavior pattern

words.name: phobia
words.description: anxiety disorder defined by a persistent and excessive fear of a concept, an object or a situation

words.name: schizophrenia
words.description: psychotic disorder characterized by emotional responsiveness and disintegration of thought process

words.name: bipolar disorder
words.description: mental illness characterized by mood changes

words.name: obsessive-compulsive disorder
words.description: mental disorder involving obsessions and compulsions

words.name: post-traumatic stress disorder
words.description: psychiatric disorder that developed after experiencing or witnessing a terrifying or life-threatening event

words.name: psychosis
words.description: abnormal condition of the mind that involves hallucinations and delusions and is a symptom of any of various disorders or other causes

words.name: anxiety
words.description: unpleasant complex combination of emotions that includes fear, apprehension and worry, and is often accompanied by physical sensations such as palpitations, nausea, chest pain and/or shortness of breath

words.name: depression
words.description: state of low mood and aversion to activity

words.name: psychotherapy
words.description: clinically applied psychology for desired behavior change

words.name: cognitive behavioral therapy
words.description: psychotherapeutic approach that addresses dysfunctional emotions, behaviors and cognitions

words.name: counseling
words.description: branch of psychology

words.name: psychoanalysis
words.description: psychological theory that was founded in 1890 by the Viennese neurologist Sigmund Freud

words.name: defence mechanism
words.description: unconscious psychological mechanism that reduces anxiety arising from unacceptable or potentially harmful stimuli

words.name: id
words.description: psychologist concepts by Sigmund Freud

words.name: unconscious mind
words.description: group of psychic characters and processes that are reflected in behaviour, even though not shown in the conciousness

words.name: synapse
words.description: junction between a nerve fiber of one neuron and another neuron, muscle fiber or glial cell

words.name: neurotransmitter
words.description: endogenous chemicals that transmit signals across a synapse from one neuron to another

words.name: dopamine
words.description: organic chemical that functions both as a hormone and a neurotransmitter

words.name: serotonin
words.description: monoamine neurotransmitter

words.name: reflex
words.description: automatic, subconscious response to a stimulus

words.name: amnesia
words.description: cognitive disorder where the memory is disturbed or lost and involves the loss of memories previously established, loss of the ability to create new memories, continuous high blood pressure and severe shock may also cause amnesia

words.name: sensation
words.description: physiological capacity of organisms that provides data for perception

words.name: absolute threshold
words.description: term in neuroscience and psychophysics

words.name: just-noticeable difference
words.description: amount that a stimulus must be changed to be detected

words.name: Weber-Fechner law
words.description: psychophysics law that the minimum perceptible increase of stimulus is proportional to the stimulus and that intensity of our sensation increases logarithmically

words.name: signal detection theory
words.description: analyzed the detection of difficult to detect signals and provides a measure of the quality of the detection

words.name: intelligence quotient
words.description: score from a test designed to assess intelligence

words.name: reliability
words.description: consistency of a measure in psychometrics and statistics, that is if it produces similar results under consistent conditions

words.name: validity
words.description: extent to which a concept, conclusion or measurement is well-founded and corresponds accurately to the real world

words.name: stress
words.description: psychological feeling of strain and pressure, that could increase the risk of strokes, heart attacks, ulcers, and mental illnesses such as depression and aggravation of a pre-existing condition

words.name: coping
words.description: strategies used during the confrontation with some events to reduce unpleasant emotions

words.name: placebo
words.description: substance or treatment of no therapeutic value

words.name: self-actualization
words.description: term used in various psychology theories

words.name: emotion
words.description: biological states associated with the nervous system

words.name: motivation
words.description: inner state causing goal-directed behavior

words.name: drive
words.description: theory that attempts to define, analyze, or classify psychological drives

words.name: instinct
words.description: inherent inclination of a living organism

words.name: need
words.description: thing that is necessary for an organism to live a healthy life

words.name: Maslow's hierarchy of needs
words.description: theory in developmental psychology proposed by Abraham Maslow, comprising a five-tier model of human needs: physiological, safety, love and belonging, esteem, and self-actualization

words.name: mood
words.description: relatively long lasting emotional, internal and subjective state

words.name: affect
words.description: experience of feeling or emotion

words.name: fear
words.description: emotion induced by perceived danger or threat

words.name: Dunbar's number
words.description: value important in sociology and anthropology

words.name: the magical number seven
words.description: 1956 thesis by George Miller on working memory capacity

refs.name: The Magical Number Seven, Plus or Minus Two

words.name: mental age
words.description: the age level at which a person performs intellectually, as measured by a standardized test

words.name: chronological age
words.description: the actual age of a person measured in years since birth

words.name: Weber fraction
words.description: the constant ratio of the just-noticeable difference to the magnitude of the original stimulus

words.name: stimulus intensity
words.description: the physical magnitude of a stimulus presented to a sense organ

words.name: perceived intensity
words.description: the subjective magnitude of a sensation as experienced by a person

words.name: scaling constant
words.description: a constant of proportionality relating a sensation to the physical stimulus that produces it

words.name: Stevens exponent
words.description: the exponent in Stevens' power law that depends on the type of stimulus being judged

words.name: raw score
words.description: an original measurement or test result before it is converted to a standardized score

words.name: standard score
words.description: the number of standard deviations by which a raw score lies above or below the mean

words.name: standard deviation
words.description: a measure of the amount of variation or dispersion of a set of values around their mean

sources.name: by definition
sources.description: a value that is fixed by an unambiguous definition or convention rather than measured

sources.name: psychology reference data
sources.description: commonly cited estimates from psychology research used as reference values
